c74object.name: dk.shattercreate~
c74object.digest: Pre-analyze a signal using Non-Negative Matrix Factorisation
c74object.description: The dk.shattercreate~ object takes audio input and decomposes it uses finely-tuned spectral contours that are optimised through machine learning. These can then be loaded into dk.shatter~ to decompose incoming audio through the same spectral contours.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithms have a look at these articles:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/nmffilter
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/nmfmatch

method.name: print
method.digest: Print summary info to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the analysis to the Max console.

method.name: seed
method.digest: Enable recording for the internal NMF decomposition buffer~
method.description: Enable recording for the internal NMF decomposition buffer~.

method.name: write
method.description: Write a .json file with all the required information to dsk.

attribute.name: components
attribute.digest: Amount of components for the internal seeding
attribute.description: Amount of components for the internal seeding (not required when loading an analysis).

attribute.name: fftsettings
attribute.description:
FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:
For the hop size, -1 = windowsize/2
For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
Constraints
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size
